nodes.x: I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.
I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: and I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.
I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: I will sing unto thee among the nations.
To the end that my glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.
I will praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
Praise ye the Lord: for it is good to sing praises unto our God; for it is pleasant; and praise is comely.
I will worship toward thy holy temple, and praise thy name for thy lovingkindness and for thy truth: for thou hast magnified thy word above all thy name.
Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
I will declare thy name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.
Therefore I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and I will sing praises unto thy name.
Saying, I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.
I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
In the day when I cried thou answeredst me, and strengthenedst me with strength in my soul.
In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.

And my soul hungered; and I kneeled down before my Maker, and I cried unto him in mighty prayer and supplication for mine own soul; and all the day long did I cry unto him; yea, and when the night came I did still raise my voice high that it reached the heavens.

And said, O man greatly beloved, fear not: peace be unto thee, be strong, yea, be strong. And when he had spoken unto me, I was strengthened, and said, Let my lord speak; for thou hast strengthened me.

How often has my mind been filled with light, and my bosom with joy, while I have thus poured out my soul, in pourtraying the blessings which pertain to the citizens of the kingdom — and as I thought had made the path of entrance so plain that “the wayfaring man, though a fool, might not err therein when every nerve of my body had been exhausted, and my voice had almost lost its tone : when…

And I cried with a loud voice,
    with the spirit of power,
    And blessed and glorified and extolled.
All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord, when they hear the words of thy mouth.
They shall speak of the glory of thy kingdom, and talk of thy power;
Hear, O ye kings; give ear, O ye princes; I, even I, will sing unto the Lord; I will sing praise to the Lord God of Israel.
O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
For he that raiseth up a king against me shall perish, for I, the Lord, the king of heaven, will be their king, and I will be a light unto them forever, that hear my words.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise, O ye servants of the Lord, praise the name of the Lord.
Yea, they shall sing in the ways of the Lord: for great is the glory of the Lord.
They shall lift up their voice, they shall sing for the majesty of the Lord, they shall cry aloud from the sea.
O come, let us sing unto the Lord: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.
They shall abundantly utter the memory of thy great goodness, and shall sing of thy righteousness.
For ye shall go out with joy, and be led forth with peace: the mountains and the hills shall break forth before you into singing, and all the trees of the field shall clap their hands.
Though the Lord be high, yet hath he respect unto the lowly: but the proud he knoweth afar off.
Who is like unto the Lord our God, who dwelleth on high,
He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.

Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
Though I walk in the midst of trouble, thou wilt revive me: thou shalt stretch forth thine hand against the wrath of mine enemies, and thy right hand shall save me.
Thou hast forsaken me, saith the Lord, thou art gone backward: therefore will I stretch out my hand against thee, and destroy thee; I am weary with repenting.
Wilt thou not revive us again: that thy people may rejoice in thee?
In the day of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou wilt answer me.
Have mercy upon me, O Lord; consider my trouble which I suffer of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:
I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
The Lord will perfect that which concerneth me: thy mercy, O Lord, endureth for ever: forsake not the works of thine own hands.
For thou, Lord, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.
O give thanks to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.
O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Have mercy, O Lord, upon his wife and children, that they may be exalted in thy presence, and preserved by thy fostering hand.
O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.
